abstract: This paper examines the load-bearing capacity of steel railway bridges dating back to the second half of the 19th century. The analysis is prompted by a study previously published in the quarterly journal *Roads and Bridges - Drogi i Mosty*, No. 2 (2014). We argue that when verifying the structural integrity of bridges that have been in service for over a century, it is essential to account for the long-term degradation of material properties and the cumulative effects of decades of operational fatigue.
